action: Ran a first structured prompt to get feedback on a piece of work usually reviewed by a colleague.
verbatim: I wasn't sure what to expect. But it actually... pushed back. In a useful way.
job_to_be_done: Get honest external feedback on work in the absence of a trusted peer.
subject: self
open_question: Is this a sustainable substitute for human feedback — or just a stopgap?

action: Asked AI to help diagnose a problem in a domain where baseline knowledge was thin — received plausible but unverifiable output.
verbatim: It gave me an answer. A confident one. I just had no way to know if it was right.
job_to_be_done: Use AI to accelerate learning in an unfamiliar domain.
subject: self
open_question: How do you evaluate AI output on a topic you don't know well enough to verify?

action: Recognised after a failed experiment that the constraint was not the tool but the absence of a clear problem definition going in.
verbatim: The tool did exactly what I asked. That was the problem. I asked the wrong thing.
job_to_be_done: Understand where AI failure begins — in the tool or in the brief.
subject: self

action: Watched a colleague present AI-generated output in a meeting without being able to defend or contextualise its contents.
verbatim: They knew the document existed. They didn't know what was in it.
job_to_be_done: Understand what it means to own output produced with AI assistance.
subject: other
open_question: When AI produces the artifact — who holds the knowledge?

action: Noticed mid-conversation that a way of framing a problem — developed through repeated AI dialogue — had permanently replaced the old one.
verbatim: I don't think I can go back to thinking about it the other way. That framing is just... gone.
job_to_be_done: Track how AI interaction changes underlying mental models over time.
subject: self
